sections.paragraph:
(with conjunctive accents) and עָם (with distinctives, or with the art.) with suff. עַמִּי, comm. (but rarely f. Exo 5:16 Jud. 18:7 ), a people, so called from their being collected together, see the root עָמַם No. 1 (Arab. عَامَّةُ the common people). It is very often used of Israel, as being the people of God, עַם יְהֹוָה Exo 15:13 Deu 32:36 עַם קָדֹושׁ the holy people, Deu 7:6 עַם נַחֲלָה the people peculiarly belonging to God, Deu 4:20, etc.; and in opposition to גֹּויִם (see גֹּוי ); but the pl. עַמִּים Isa 8:9 Psal. 33:10 , and עַמֵּי הָאָרֶץ Deu 28:10, etc., is used of all peoples. Specially it is used-

(1) of single races or tribes, e.g. עַם זְבֻלוּן Jud. 5:18 pl. often of the tribes of Israel (comp. the δήμοι of the Athenians), Gen 49:10 Deu 32:8 33:3 , 19 Deu 33:19 Isa 3:13 Hos 10:14 Psa 47:2, 10 Ps4 7:10and even used of the race or family of any one, especially in the plural עַמֵּי פ׳ the kindred, relatives of any one, i.q. אַנְשֵׁי פ׳ (see אִישׁ No. 1, ) Lev 21:1, Lev 21:4 19:16 . נֶאֱסַף אֶל עַמָּיו to be gathered to one’s people, i.q. elsewhere is called, to be gathered to one’s fathers (see אָסַף Niphal). (Hence has arisen its use in the singular of single relatives; whence Arab. عَمُّ an uncle, and the pr.n. עַמִּיאֵל kinsman of God, אֱלִיעָם to whom God is kinsman.) Poet. used of any peculiar race of men, as עַם עָנִי the afflicted people, Psa 18:28 comp. גֹּוי צַדִּיק just men, Gen 20:4.

(2) Opp. to princes, leaders, or the king; it denotes the citizens, the common people (compare λαός opp. to leaders, Il. ii. 365; xiii. 108; xxiv. 28), 1Ki 12:16 2Ki 11:17 23:21 Eze 7:27 soldiers, Jud. 5:2 hence, followed by a genit. the companions, or servants of a leader or lord; i.q. אֲנָשָׁיו (see אִישׁ No. 1, ); feine Leute. Son 6:12, מַרְכְּבֹות עַמִּי נָדִיב “the chariot of the companions of the prince;” die Wagen des fürftlichen Gefolges: (־ִי being, I consider, in this place not a suffix, but paragogic, and a mark of the constr. state). Ecc 4:16 also used of the servants of a private master, 1Ki 19:21 2Ki 4:41. Elsewhere-

(3) when an individual speaks, my people is the people to which I belong; Isa 53:8 [?]; Rth 1:16 whence בְּנֵי עַמִּי the sons of my people; i.e. my countrymen, Gen 23:11 poet. בַּת עַמִּי id. (see בַּת No. 5 ), Lam 2:11 3:14 4:3 , . With the art. it is used-

(4) also of the whole human race, i.q. הָאָדָם Isa 40:7 42:5 44:7 and to this may also be referred the words spoken in bitter irony, Job 12:2, אָמְנָם כִּי אַתֶּם עָם “surely ye are the whole human race, and with you wisdom will die,” (ihr fehd alle Welt, und habt aller Welt Weisheit).

(5) Poet. used of a troop, herd of animals, Pro 30:25, 26 Pro 30:26 Psa 74:14 compare גֹּוי No. 2; also Gr. δῆμος.

Plur. עַמִּים constr. עַמֵּי (more rarely in the Aramean manner עֲמָמִים constr. עַמְמֵי Neh 9:22, 24 Jud. 5:14 ); peoples, nations; also the tribes of Israel; see above No. 1, the kindred, relatives of any one; see above No. 2.

עם

Chald. id. Plur. עַמְמִין, emph. עַמְמַיָּא Dan 3:4, , 31 5:19 6:26 7:14 . Syriac ܥܰܘܠܐܳ; plur. ܥܰܡ̈ܡ̱ܐܶ.
